description: mailc places content directly in column td without per-component tr>td wrappers
reason: MJML wraps every component in tr>td with font-size:0px, padding, and word-break. mailc handles word-break at the column td level — less HTML, same rendering.
mailcApproach: Button markup directly in column td — no per-component wrapper

description: mailc uses VML v:roundrect for Outlook buttons instead of MJML's table+bgcolor approach
reason: MJML uses a table wrapper with bgcolor for Outlook background and mso-padding-alt. mailc uses VML v:roundrect giving pixel-perfect rounded buttons in Outlook — better rendering, less HTML.

description:
mailc uses semantic

with margin:0 instead of MJML's

wrapper with injected font defaults
reason: MJML uses div because its own CSS reset breaks p elements. mailc uses p directly with margin:0 — semantically correct, less HTML.
mailcApproach:
text

— semantically correct, no font defaults injected

description: Text content extraction order differs due to title and preview text DOM placement
reason: Both compilers include the same visible text. Difference is DOM ordering — affects flat extraction only, not rendering.
mjmlApproach: <title>text</title> in head, preview text with zero-width padding
mailcApproach: <title>text</title> in head, preview text in hidden div — same content, different order

description: MJML button td uses bgcolor HTML attr; mailc VML v:roundrect handles Outlook colors natively
reason: The bgcolor attr is exclusively on button <td> elements. mailc uses VML v:roundrect which provides pixel-perfect rounded corners AND background colors in Outlook — the superior approach.
